description: Dockerfile security and best-practice linting agent fixture: root user detection, ENV secret exposure, clean approval (false-positive avoidance), ADD vs COPY best practice, multi-stage SSH key leak, and no-content error handling. All Dockerfiles are synthetic.
notes: risk_tier=high: false-negative on a secret-in-ENV or root-user finding is a high-severity evaluation failure
Case 3 (clean-approval) uses golden evaluator at threshold 0.70 — primary signal is approval without fabricated findings
Case 5 (multi-stage-secret-leak) tests intermediate layer awareness — agents that only check the final stage will fail
All credential-like strings (DATABASE_URL, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) in fixtures are synthetic and do not represent real secrets
Hashes: re-run `agentcarousel bundle pack fixtures/bundles/dockerfile-linter` after file changes
open_items: OI-001: Add judge evaluator case for nuanced BuildKit --mount=type=secret guidance
OI-002: Advance to stable certification track after 5 carousel runs with <10% rubric variance
